claim_boundary.supported_by_this_artifact: TideFS has a source-reviewed, issue-gated validation matrix for VFS_RPC forwarding evidence, including the smallest focused workflow and artifact surface for each forwarding claim row.
not_supported_by_this_artifact: VFS_RPC inline forwarding is not runtime-validated by this artifact.
BULK-bound VFS_RPC data movement is not validated while #1523 remains open; #1550 landed the BULK service surface but not the VFS_RPC handoff binding.
RDMA readiness is not claimed; RDMA remains behind BULK, transport peer security, pinned-memory accounting, rkey/addr lifecycle, abort cleanup, and hardware/runtime evidence gates.
Multi-node product readiness is not claimed.
Storage semantics or durability authority is not claimed.

live_prerequisites.role: selected the VFS_RPC control/inline forwarding boundary with BULK-plane data movement and explicit non-claims

current_source_evidence: crates/tidefs-vfs-rpc/src/transport_adapter.rs binds service 0x06 to the CONTROL path
transport adapter tests cover frame limits, peer/session errors, retry/timeout surfaces, peer mismatch, and BULK-not-bound rejection

live_prerequisites.role: VFS Engine bridge prerequisite for inline forwarding, stale term/epoch rejection, dedup replay, credential mismatch, and transferable handle rejection rows
live_prerequisites.current_gate: Issue #1522 records a source-edit gate for bridge module/dependency wiring before bridge behavior can land.

live_prerequisites.role: BULK service and InlineOrBulk/BulkToken handoff prerequisite
live_prerequisites.current_gate: PR #1550 is merged for the TCP token handoff service surface. Issue #1523 remains open for transport/VFS_RPC integration: service_id 0x07 frame dispatch, same-authenticated-connection peer binding, and VFS_RPC timeout/ABORT notification. This branch treats BULK-bound forwarding rows as gated until that integration lands and is validated.

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: VFS_RPC request and response frames for service id 0x06 are wrapped and unwrapped through the transport CONTROL envelope without changing the VFS_RPC wire protocol.

smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence for transport framing, service id, CONTROL lane selection, frame-size rejection, and response correlation only

smallest_workflows.ref: feature branch carrying the evidence map
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: schema evidence that the source-controlled VFS_RPC forwarding evidence map is a bounded claim-boundary artifact

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: Validated inline VFS_RPC operations are forwarded to writer-side VFS Engine dispatch and return typed VFS_RPC responses without changing storage semantics.

smallest_workflows.ref: #1522 implementation branch after the bridge prerequisite lands
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence for inline bridge dispatch and typed response encoding only

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: Forwarded operations reject stale writer, term, or epoch input with ESTALE or a documented retry signal before side effects.

smallest_workflows.ref: #1522 implementation branch after writer lease validation exists
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence for bridge-side stale writer, term, and epoch refusal only

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: Per-peer op_id dedup replays completed mutation responses, evicts by the bounded window, and honors REQ_FLAG_NO_DEDUP for one-shot paths.

smallest_workflows.ref: #1522 implementation branch after bridge dispatch can consume the existing VFS_RPC dedup window
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence for completed-response replay, window eviction, and NO_DEDUP bypass at the bridge boundary only

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: The transport-authenticated peer identity is checked against VfsRpcCredentials before handles or operations are accepted; mismatch returns EACCES.

smallest_workflows.ref: #1522 implementation branch after bridge credential admission exists
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence for peer credential mismatch refusal only

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: Transferable file and directory handles are rejected for wrong writer, dataset, inode generation, missing cookie, released handle, or peer mismatch.

smallest_workflows.ref: #1522 implementation branch after handle resolution exists
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence for scoped transferable-handle rejection only

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: Before VFS_RPC binds a live BULK handoff, it rejects or defers REQ_FLAG_BULK_PENDING, RESP_FLAG_BULK, and InlineOrBulk::Bulk descriptors instead of truncating, inlining, or claiming success.

smallest_workflows.ref: feature branch under test before the #1523 VFS_RPC/BULK integration lands
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence that BULK-not-bound descriptors fail closed at the transport adapter boundary only

minimum_validation_matrix.claim: After VFS_RPC binds the live TCP_STREAM BULK handoff, VFS_RPC WRITE waits for same-connection BULK DONE before engine dispatch and READ publishes a same-connection BulkToken without treating RDMA or moved bytes as storage semantics proof.

smallest_workflows.ref: branch that contains the #1522 bridge and #1523 VFS_RPC/BULK integration on top of the merged BULK service surface
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: cargo-unit evidence for TCP_STREAM BulkToken handoff, failed-transfer discard, and retry/op_id interaction only

smallest_workflows.ref: branch carrying the post-BULK evidence manifest
smallest_workflows.artifact_claim: schema evidence for a future BULK-bound forwarding artifact; not RDMA, multi-node, release-candidate, or storage semantics authority

publication_requirements.run_url_surface: PR body or issue comment for #1524
required_for_each_run: branch or PR ref under test
uploaded artifact name or explicit no-upload summary
exact claim supported by that artifact
